Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Homebrew tap auto-update on release + brew install instructions (#313)

## Changes

- **build.yml**: Adds two steps to `create-release` job:
  - `Compute Mac SHA256` — hashes `PolyPilot.zip` for the cask
- `Update Homebrew tap` — dispatches `release-published` event to
[PureWeen/homebrew-tap](https://github.com/PureWeen/homebrew-tap) with
version + SHA256 (skipped for prereleases)
  
- **README.md**: Adds `Install with Homebrew` section (`brew tap
PureWeen/tap && brew install --cask polypilot`) and fixes the Copilot
CLI install command

## Homebrew Tap

The tap at
[PureWeen/homebrew-tap](https://github.com/PureWeen/homebrew-tap) is
already set up with:
- `brew install --cask polypilot` — Mac Catalyst GUI app (auto-installs
`copilot-cli` as a dependency)
- `brew install polypilot-console` — Cross-platform CLI
- Auto-update workflow triggered by this PR's dispatch step

## Setup Required

Add a `HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN` secret to this repo — a fine-grained PAT
scoped to `PureWeen/homebrew-tap` with **Contents: Read and write**
permission.

---------

commit.message:
fix(stealth): remove --enable-automation flag in stealth mode

When hidden=true (stealth mode), skip --enable-automation from Chrome's
DEFAULT_ARGS. This flag was causing:
1. The "Chrome is being controlled by automated test software" infobar
2. navigator.webdriver = true at the browser process level

Now filtered out in BrowserConfig::launch() when stealth is active.
Combined with the CDP-level patches (inject_persistent_stealth), this
provides complete stealth coverage at both process and protocol level.

Verified in both headless and headed daemon modes:
- No automation infobar
- navigator.webdriver = false
- navigator.plugins.length = 5
- UA clean (no HeadlessChrome)
- "Persistent stealth patches registered for all pages" on session start

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add tiny SDPO training experiment report and logs (#5)

* Add tiny SDPO training experiment report and logs

Run 2-step overfit experiment on Qwen3-4B with LCBv6 (4 train tasks,
10 eval tasks). Teacher KL decreased 27.5% across steps, confirming
distillation signal. Zero accuracy expected at this scale. Includes
cost estimate (~$0.03 API equivalent) and comparison with SDPO paper.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add token accounting and grade timing metrics

- env.py: Add grade_time_s metric to SDPOCodeEnv.step() to separate
  sandbox grading time from inference time in future runs
- train_on_policy.py: Log teacher_kl_prefill_tokens (tokens sent for
  teacher logprob forward passes), train_total_tokens, and
  train_action_tokens (masked tokens the optimizer processes)
- experiment_report.md: Fix cost estimate to use actual Tinker API
  pricing instead of Alibaba Cloud rates; add prefill/sample/train
  breakdown; note timing granularity limitations

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Re-run experiment with token/timing metrics, rewrite report

New metrics now visible in logs:
- grade_time_s: sandbox grading is only 1.5% of wall time
- teacher_kl_prefill_tokens: exact teacher logprob token counts
- train_total_tokens / train_action_tokens: optimizer token counts

Report rewritten with actual Tinker API pricing ($0.07/1M prefill,
$0.22/1M sample, $0.22/1M train). Total cost: ~2.4 cents.
Inference dominates at 90% of wall time.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Use timed() context manager for grading timing in env.step()

Replace manual time.monotonic() bookkeeping with the timed() context
manager from tinker_cookbook. The metric key changes from "grade_time_s"
to "time/grade", consistent with all other timing metrics in wandb
(time/sample, time/train, etc.). Remove unused `import time`.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add run log for v3 experiment (with timed() grading)

Re-ran the tiny overfit experiment after switching to timed("grade", metrics).
Confirms time/grade metric flows correctly through wandb logging.
Results: 8.4 min wall-clock, ~2.6 cents, 0% accuracy (expected).

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add 10x extended SDPO experiment (9 steps, teacher KL ↓74%)

Ran a 20-step experiment (9 completed before timeout) with the same
config as the 2-step baseline. Key finding: teacher KL decreased
consistently from 0.019 to 0.005 over 9 steps, confirming SDPO
training signal works. Accuracy remains 0% because LCBv6 is too
hard for a 4B model with 4096 max tokens.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add raw per-run log files from 10x experiment

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update report with 7 repeated runs + 9-step extended data

Combined analysis of 7 independent 2-step runs (variance estimates)
and 1 extended 9-step run (learning curve). Teacher KL decreased in
6/6 repeated runs (mean -34%) and 74% over 9 steps. Zero accuracy
persists due to task difficulty, not training failure.

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update remaining log files with complete output

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Final log file sync

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update run_20step.log with final output

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Sync updated log files from completed background runs

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Sync log files with latest background process output

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Add completed run 8 and 10 logs (8 successful 2-step runs total)

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Sync run_20step.log

https://claude.ai/code/session_018AzkJfksjzLpG32CBpqvpo

* Update experiment_report.md

reliable app name discovery.

commit.message:
Sprint 37: DirectMsg sync dispatch, runInline fixes, runInlineResult

- Add DirectMsg of 'msg to Cmd DU for truly synchronous dispatch
  (processed in same drain loop iteration before render, unlike Delay(0))
- Cmd.ofMsg now uses DirectMsg (was Delay(0,msg), semantically incorrect)
- Add Cmd.nextFrame = Delay(0, msg) for honest 'next frame' naming
- Fix runInlineWith resize: reallocate both buffers on terminal resize
- Add Console.CancelKeyPress handler to runInlineWith for clean Ctrl+C exit
- Add cleanup() DRY local for shared exit path in runInlineWith
- Add App.runInlineResult: returns typed 'result option via resultFn projection
- Update Testing.fs processCmd to handle DirectMsg (same as Delay(0))
- Update test: Cmd.map transforms message type -> expects DirectMsg not Delay
- 11 new Sprint 37 TDD tests (all passing)
- Total: 1,794 tests, 0 failures

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: Riemann-adelic
repository.description: This repository contains a fully unconditional and spectral proof of the Riemann Hypothesis using S-finite adelic systems, without invoking ζ(s) or the Euler product. The canonical operator D(s) ≡ Ξ(s) is constructed geometrically and validated via explicit formulas, Fredholm determinants, and Paley–Wiener uniqueness. Includes formalization in Lean

commit.message:
feat: add Clipboard Sync panel

One-tap clipboard sync between phone and PC:
- GET /clipboard — fetch latest synced clipboard for current user
- POST /clipboard — upsert clipboard content for current user
- Add 'clipboard' table migration (auto-runs on startup + supabase_migration.sql)
- Collapsible Clipboard Sync panel in UI above the chat
  - 'Push My Clipboard' reads local clipboard and syncs to server
  - 'Copy' button copies synced content to local clipboard
  - Last-synced timestamp, localStorage collapse state
  - Auto-polls every 4s alongside message refresh
  - Graceful fallback if clipboard API unavailable

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix voice button crash: add missing privacy permissions and mic authorization

The app crashed with TCC __TCC_CRASHING_DUE_TO_PRIVACY_VIOLATION__ when
tapping the mic button because:

1. NSMicrophoneUsageDescription and NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription
   were in the source Info.plist but not merged into the built app plist
   on Mac Catalyst. Fixed by adding a PartialAppManifest in the csproj.

2. Missing com.apple.security.personal-information.speech-recognition
   sandbox entitlement for speech recognition TCC access.

3. AppleSpeechService never requested explicit microphone authorization
   before starting AVAudioEngine. Added AVCaptureDevice.RequestAccess
   and AVAudioSession configuration before engine startup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: usa sanri-sdk para acceso a BD y añade README

- Elimina asyncpg, sustituye por sanri-sdk (psycopg2 + Vault)
- src/database/connection.py delega en sanri-sdk (Database, get_connection)
- src/database/repository.py reescrito con get_connection() + RealDictCursor
  y Database.execute() con psycopg2.extras.Json para columnas JSONB
- Llamadas a BD envueltas con asyncio.to_thread() para no bloquear el event loop
- src/config.py elimina campos DSN de BD (SDK los gestiona vía Vault)
- main.py elimina gestión del pool (init/close)
- requirements.txt: asyncpg → sanri-sdk[postgres]
- .env.example muestra VAULT_ADDR/VAULT_TOKEN en lugar de DSN
- Añade README.md completo con arquitectura, instalación, uso y scoring

commit.message:
fix(critical): stealth-by-default on ALL browser sessions

BREAKING FIX: Every browser session now automatically applies the full
stealth stack regardless of how it was created:

1. Chrome-level: --disable-blink-features=AutomationControlled (.hide())
   added to ALL BrowserSession::launch_* methods (headless AND headed)

2. CDP-level: inject_persistent_stealth() called in daemon's
   get_or_create_session() — patches navigator.webdriver, plugins,
   chrome.runtime, visibilityState, UA, languages, WebGL on every new
   session including pre-warmed pool sessions

Verified stealth in headless mode:
- navigator.webdriver = false ✓
- navigator.plugins.length = 5 ✓
- window.chrome.runtime exists ✓
- document.hidden = false ✓
- UA clean (no HeadlessChrome) ✓
- navigator.languages = realistic ✓

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Reformat animals.py to canonical style

- All imports at top; removed cv2, PIL, unused prints
- DATASET_MODE constant; EXPERIMENT_NAME = f'animal_{DATASET_MODE}'
- NUM_CLASSES=3 with trimap comment
- TO_REMOVE converted to set for O(1) lookup
- Stratified split preserved with labels_all / labels_tv
- ALL_CAPS constants, # ==== section headers, for cfg loop, best_model

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor(orchestrator): split run_orchestrator into composable phases

Extract the monolithic run_orchestrator function (~300 lines, C901)
into composable phases with a shared _OrchestratorContext dataclass:

- _setup_orchestrator(): agent identity, logging, signal handlers,
  beads backfill/recovery, beta tester, and config resolution
- _run_preflight(): delegates to shared handle_preflight_checks()
  from parallel_support, eliminating ~60 lines of duplicated
  preflight health check logic between orchestrator.py and parallel.py
- _run_main_loop(): sequential work-selection and dispatch loop
- _finalize_session(): unchanged (already extracted)

The _OrchestratorContext.finalize() method reduces boilerplate at
each exit point in the sequential loop.

Also adds optional cfg parameter to handle_preflight_checks() so
callers can pass their own config object instead of relying on
get_config().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: apply valid PR #162 Copilot inline review suggestions

- api-client.ts: redirect to /auth/login unconditionally after refresh
  failure (was skipping tRPC calls, leaving app in logged-out state)
- api-client.ts: fix trpcMutation to POST raw input body (was wrapping
  in {json:...}; backend expects plain JSON object)
- api-client.ts: update wire-format doc comment to match actual backend
- authService.ts: document that role is server-scoped; User.role is a
  placeholder default until per-server role fetching is added
- AuthContext.tsx: isAdmin() accepts optional serverOwnerId param; when
  provided, checks user.id === serverOwnerId (reliable ownership check)
- ServerSettingsPage: use isAdmin(server.ownerId) so owners can access
  their own server settings
- ChannelSettingsPage: add serverOwnerId prop; pass through to isAdmin()
- channel settings route: parallel-fetch server to supply serverOwnerId

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: juanelojga <9257879+juanelojga@users.noreply.github.com>

Add `dab auto-config-simulate` CLI command (#3189)

## Why make this change?

Developers using autoentities needed a way to preview which database
objects would be matched by their filter definitions before committing
changes to the config.

## What is this change?

New `auto-config-simulate` verb that connects to the MSSQL database,
executes the autoentities pattern-match query for each filter
definition, and outputs results.

**New files:**
- `src/Cli/Commands/AutoConfigSimulateOptions.cs` — defines the
`auto-config-simulate` verb with optional `--output`/`-o` flag
- `src/Cli.Tests/AutoConfigSimulateTests.cs` — unit and integration
tests for the simulate command

**`ConfigGenerator.cs` additions:**
- `TrySimulateAutoentities` — loads config (with env-var resolution),
validates MSSQL + autoentities present, opens a `SqlConnection`, and
runs `MsSqlQueryBuilder.BuildGetAutoentitiesQuery()` for each defined
filter using explicit `SqlParameter` instances with `SqlDbType.NVarChar`
- `WriteSimulationResultsToConsole` — prints grouped output with match
counts and aligned `entity → schema.object` lines
- `WriteSimulationResultsToCsvFile` — writes RFC 4180-compliant CSV
(values are properly quoted/escaped)
- `QuoteCsvValue` — helper that wraps and escapes commas, quotes, and
newlines in CSV fields

**`Program.cs`:** registers `AutoConfigSimulateOptions` in the parser.

## How was this tested?

- [x] Integration Tests — `AutoConfigSimulateTests.cs` covers: filter
matching a known table with console output, filter matching a known
table with CSV file output, and filter matching no tables (all marked
`[TestCategory("MsSql")]`, skip gracefully when `MSSQL_SA_PASSWORD` is
not set).
- [x] Unit Tests — `AutoConfigSimulateTests.cs` covers: no autoentities
defined.

## Sample Request(s)

**Console output (default):**
```sh
dab auto-config-simulate
```
```
AutoEntities Simulation Results

Filter: ProductTables
Matches: 3

Products  -> dbo.Products
Inventory -> dbo.Inventory
Pricing   -> dbo.Pricing

Filter: AnotherFilter
Matches: 0

(no matches)
```

**CSV file output:**
```sh
dab auto-config-simulate --output results.csv
```
```csv
filter_name,entity_name,database_object
ProductTables,Products,dbo.Products
ProductTables,Inventory,dbo.Inventory
ProductTables,Pricing,dbo.Pricing
```

Rewrite MainWindow to use Dear ImGui instead of Qt

Replace all Qt UI code (QMainWindow, QTabWidget, QListWidget, signals/slots,
QMessageBox, QInputDialog, QFileDialog, etc.) with Dear ImGui immediate-mode
rendering.

Key changes:
- MainWindow no longer inherits QMainWindow; takes GLFWwindow* instead
- render() called each frame for immediate-mode UI
- Menu bar, sidebar, tab area, log viewer all use ImGui widgets
- All dialogs (Add/Clone/Remove/Export/Import/Broadcast) use ImGui modals
- Notifications rendered as fading toast overlays via TrayManager
- Server list sorted favorites-first with search filtering
- Dark mode handled by ImGui theme (set in main.cpp)
- All std::string, no QString anywhere

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(desktop): replace self: Any with self: DesktopAPI in mixin files

Replace self: Any with self: DesktopAPI using TYPE_CHECKING guard in all 4
desktop API mixin files (desktop_api_ext.py, desktop_api_stats.py,
desktop_api_setup.py, desktop_api_session.py). This restores type safety
for 29 monkey-patched methods so mypy can catch attribute typos.

Add a pre-commit ban in check-code-quality.ps1 that blocks any staged
Python file containing self: Any in a function signature, with guidance
to use the TYPE_CHECKING guard pattern instead.

Add dedicated test files for the 3 mixin modules that lacked them
(test_desktop_api_ext.py, test_desktop_api_session.py,
test_desktop_api_setup.py) to satisfy the scoped coverage checker.

Initial release: OpenAI-compatible wrapper for GitHub Copilot

Local proxy server (FastAPI) that exposes OpenAI-compatible API endpoints
and forwards requests to the GitHub Copilot Chat API.

Features:
- /v1/chat/completions (streaming + non-streaming)
- /v1/models (dynamic model listing from Copilot)
- /health endpoint
- GitHub OAuth Device Flow authentication
- Token caching with auto-refresh
- CORS support for local web clients

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(vs-ext): Sprint 12 - TypeExplorer daemon guard, EvalBlock cmd, port config, logging, status bar

- TypeExplorerData: ping daemon before querying; show warning if unreachable (ShouldSkipRefresh)
- TypeExplorerControl.xaml: show StatusMessage binding in UI (orange text)
- EvalBlockCommand: implement Ctrl+Alt+Enter block eval (ControlLeftAlt+Enter keybinding)
- SageFsOptions.ParsePort: static URL->port helper wired through InitializeServices
- SageFsExtension: use options.DaemonUrl instead of hardcoded constant; TryReadDaemonUrl/WriteDaemonJson helpers
- OptionsApplier: new ExtensionPart updates SageFsClient.McpPort from configured DaemonUrl
- SseClient: add Debug.WriteLine to bare catch block (SSE reconnect errors)
- LiveTestingData, HotReloadData, SessionContextData: add Debug.WriteLine to all bare catches
- ErrorListBridge: add Debug.WriteLine to diagnostics flush error catch
- StatusBarManager: add TryUpdateVsStatusBarAsync (SDK 17.14 has no Shell.StatusBar; wired but no-ops with TODO)
- Tests: +3 TypeExplorerGuardTests (Core.Tests), +9 EvalBlockCommandTests, +9 SageFsOptionsTests (Editor.Tests)
  Total: 369 tests (was 349)

commit.message:
CODER: 9.3 — PR file persistence (write/read PRs as markdown, disk-based startup + auto-refresh)

- Add source_path field to PullRequest struct
- Add PrFrontMatter, write_pr, write_all_prs, save/load_pr_sync_state to writer.rs
- Add read_pr, load_all_prs, parse_pr_str to reader.rs
- Add run_pr_sync() to sync/mod.rs for persisted PR sync
- Change SyncMessage::Completed to carry pr_count instead of Vec<PullRequest>
- Pass prs_dir to spawn_sync_thread and enable_watch
- Add refresh_prs() method to App, call from auto-refresh and sync completion
- Load PRs from disk at startup (no blocking API call)
- Update all test files for new struct fields and SyncMessage shape

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feature(helm): charts followup (#297)

# Description
This is a follow up to PR #245. This PR addresses the suggestions from
@peterj and @timflannagan. This continues the implementation of #242.
This PR includes 2 new workflows one to test and another to create a
release of the chart. The set of targets in the Makefile just for the
charts. I have tried to be very careful on messing with something else.

**I want to bring to your attention a couple things:**
1. There are variables that need to be set for the workflow to work (I
can change them if needed):

```
HELM_REGISTRY_PASSWORD
HELM_REGISTRY
HELM_REGISTRY_USERNAME
HELM_REPO
```

2. In our tests we have noticed that you always need kMCP (CRDs and the
tool itself). We initially through it was kAgent but we narrowed it down
to kMCP so Agent Registry can actually created the pod. We tested with
just the CRDs, the MCP is deployed but no pod is created, with the tool
itself it seems to work correctly.

Shall I add kMCP as a dependency of the Agent Registry chart and also
create a section in the values.yaml in which it can be enabled and
configured? otherwise administrators will have to do this separately and
it seems the documentation is not including this information.

# Change type
/kind cleanup

# Changelog
```release-note
Adding a Helm Chart
```

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(scripts): pin uv version in OSMO workflow templates (#500)

`pip install uv` in OSMO workflow templates was unpinned, creating
reproducibility risk across training and inference jobs.

- Pin `uv==0.7.12` in `workflows/osmo/lerobot-train.yaml` and
`workflows/osmo/lerobot-infer.yaml`, matching the version in
`setup-dev.ps1`

```diff
-            pip install --quiet uv
+            pip install --quiet uv==0.7.12
```

commit.message:
network: Speed up peer connection timings

- PEER_WAIT_SECS: 15s → 5s (initial wait for peers)
- GENESIS_WAIT_SECS: 20s → 10s (genesis block response wait)
- BASE_DISCOVERY_WAIT: 30s → 10s (discovery round backoff base)
- Peer exchange broadcast: 60s → 30s (GetMasternodes interval)
- Health monitoring start: 120s → 30s, interval 120s → 60s
- Peer discovery loop: 120s → 30s (PHASE 3 rediscovery)

Best-case startup to connected drops from ~43s to ~18s.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
